title: Warm Molten Brazilian White Fish Skillet
description: A savory and romantic Brazilian dish featuring white fish in a molten skillet, perfect for a special occasion, this lacto-vegetarian friendly recipe is sure to impress.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, until fragrant.
Add the white fish fillets and cook for 3-4 minutes per side, until cooked through.
Remove the fish from the skillet and set aside.
Add the coconut milk to the skillet and stir to deglaze, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom.
Bring the coconut milk to a simmer and cook until slightly reduced, about 2 minutes.
Stir in the dried thyme, salt, and pepper.
Return the fish to the skillet and spoon some of the coconut milk sauce over the top of each fillet.
Sprinkle the shredded cheese over the top of the fish and cook until melted and bubbly.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and serve immediately.
